notes:
Ember 1.9.7 — the previous five releases (1.9.2-1.9.6) never actually published due to a release-pipeline bug — this build includes everything from all of them, plus a workflow-replay crash fix

• Playing back a recorded workflow could crash Ember entirely ("Ember quit unexpectedly") on macOS — replay simulated input via pynput's Controller, which shares the same low-level macOS event machinery as pynput's Listener (already known to hard-crash off the main thread). Replay now uses pyautogui instead, the same mechanism Ember's own AI mouse/keyboard control already relies on safely.
• Fixed the release pipeline itself: a Linux packaging hiccup was silently discarding already-successful macOS/Windows builds, so nothing published for several versions in a row.
• Natural voice (Gemini Live API) was failing every connection with a policy-violation error — the model ID had been retired by Google; updated, with a fallback chain for future retirements.
• Ember Browser failed to load with a QtWebChannel error — a required component was accidentally excluded from the packaged app.
• Ember will no longer invent a placeholder email address (like name@example.com) when it doesn't know a contact's real one.
• 'Lock PC' could silently just sleep the display instead of actually locking the session; added a 'Mic On' button (there was only 'Mic Off'); the Ad Blocker now explains the Secure DNS limitation.
• Fixed 'Sandbox'/'Scan a folder' Command Center buttons, the Ember Link mirror rendering too small, added a tablet-style control bar to Ember Link fullscreen, and defaulted Ember Link to a lighter/faster quality tier.
• Ember now explicitly asks for Screen Recording and Microphone permission shortly after launch.
• Ember Link's PIN could regenerate itself unexpectedly after a restart; two-finger scroll now works on Ember Link; Ember Browser's ad blocking now shares the system-wide blocklist instead of a separate, much smaller one.
